# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our Python web application. Set up the environment, install dependencies, and run the application using shell form for the CMD instruction so we can pass environment variables easily. Example CMD python app.py $PORT

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Python runtime as a parent image
FROM python:3.8-slim-buster

# Set the working directory in the container at /app
WORKDIR /app 

# Add requirements file to docker build context and install dependencies using pip
COPY requirements*.txt ./   # Copying all requirement files into Docker image for better management of multiple dependency versions.
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt    # Install python packages specified in the requiremnts.* txt file 
# Add .dockerignore to ignore untracked files, so that we can add only necessary source code and dependencies into Docker image  
COPY ./.dockerignore ./    
RUN pip3 install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt    # Install python packages specified in the requiremnts.* txt file 
# Copy all content from current directory to /app inside docker container, so that we can access source code and dependencies directly within Docker image  
COPY . ./    
RUN chmod +x app.py      # Make sure our application is executable by running it with shell form for CMD instruction   
CMD ["python3", "./app.py"]  # Run the python script using command line arguments passed to docker run, so that we can pass environment variables easily
